decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing aggressive creatures paired with disruptive elements to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By combining efficient creatures like Thalia, Guardian of Thraben and Stoneforge Mystic with powerful removal spells like Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to apply pressure while keeping the opponent off balance. The inclusion of Aether Vial allows for explosive plays and flexibility in deploying creatures, while the presence of cards like Karakas and Wasteland provides additional disruption. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression and disruption.

This Aluren combo deck focuses on quickly getting Aluren onto the battlefield to cast creatures like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Formidable Speaker for free. The deck also includes cards like Show and Tell and Omniscience to cheat out powerful cards like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Acererak the Archlich. The deck is built to generate overwhelming advantage and close out the game quickly once the combo is assembled. The mana base includes cards like Lotus Petal, City of Traitors, and Ancient Tomb to help ramp into the combo pieces efficiently.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing lands as its main resource. By using cards like Exploration, Life from the Loam, and Crop Rotation, the deck can quickly assemble powerful land combinations such as Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. It also includes disruptive elements like Wasteland and Pithing Needle to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The key strategy is to efficiently manage and utilize lands to gain an advantage over the opponent, while also having answers to threats through cards like Swords to Plowshares and The Tabernacle at Pendrell Vale.

The deck focuses on quickly getting creatures into the graveyard and then reanimating them for powerful effects. It includes cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead to bring creatures back from the graveyard, as well as creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Atraxa, Grand Unifier with strong abilities. The deck also has control elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to disrupt opponents' plans and protect its own strategy. The combination of graveyard manipulation and creature reanimation allows for explosive plays and powerful threats to overwhelm opponents.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly put artifact creatures into play and utilize various artifacts to generate mana and disrupt the opponent's game plan. By combining aggressive creatures like Patchwork Automaton and Kappa Cannoneer with powerful artifacts like Urza's Saga and Mox Opal, the deck aims to pressure the opponent and control the board. Additionally, cards like Emry, Lurker of the Loch and Force of Will provide versatility and card advantage to maintain momentum. Overall, the deck focuses on creating a fast and disruptive game plan using artifacts in a Legacy 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures like Guide of Souls and Ocelot Pride while controlling the board with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Static Prison. The deck also utilizes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to further push the aggro strategy. The mana base is designed to support the aggressive game plan with fetchlands like Arid Mesa and efficient dual lands like Plateau. Overall, the key concept is to establish early pressure and maintain control of the battlefield to secure a quick victory.

The main idea of this deck is to control the game through the use of lands that disrupt your opponent's strategy while also building up your own game plan. By utilizing cards like Life from the Loam, Exploration, and Crop Rotation, you can quickly assemble powerful land combos such as Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage. Cards like Sphere of Resistance and Mox Diamond help you slow down your opponent's plays, while Crucible of Worlds and Wasteland give you repeated land destruction. Overall, this deck aims to outmaneuver and outlast your opponent through strategic land plays.

The deck focuses on utilizing cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack to cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield early. The deck also includes control elements like Force of Will and Spell Pierce to protect the combo. By ramping up quickly with Lotus Petal and Ancient Tomb, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with massive threats and win the game before the opponent has a chance to stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing the Aluren combo to play creatures for free, leading to a powerful win condition. By casting Aluren and then using cards like Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Formidable Speaker along with free spells like Show and Tell and Omniscience, the deck can quickly overwhelm opponents with powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. The deck also includes disruptive spells like Force of Will to protect its combo pieces and ensure its success. The deck aims to efficiently and aggressively assemble its combo to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing counterspells, removal spells, and disruptive creatures. By using cards like Force of Will, Stifle, and Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while advancing its own with powerful planeswalkers like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Teferi, Time Raveler. The deck also aims to ramp up its mana with cards like Up the Beanstalk and Quantum Riddler to cast big threats like Murktide Regent. Overall, the deck's strategy is to maintain control of the game while setting up for a powerful finish.

This Boros Aggro deck focuses on using efficient creatures and removal spells to apply constant pressure on the opponent. The deck also utilizes Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion to provide additional value with its flicker ability. By combining powerful creatures like Stoneforge Mystic and Guide of Souls with disruptive spells like Solitude and Swords to Plowshares, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent and control the board while maintaining a fast-paced aggressive strategy. The inclusion of cards like Aether Vial and Karakas also helps to provide flexibility and resilience in various matchups.

The main concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly ramp up mana using efficient lands like Eldrazi Temple and Ancient Tomb, while disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful Eldrazi creatures like Reality Smasher and It That Heralds the End, backed up by cards like Glaring Fleshraker for control and Sire of Seven Deaths for additional late-game power. The inclusion of Eye of Ugin allows for additional searching and summoning of Eldrazi creatures to maintain pressure on the opponent.

The main idea of this deck is to quickly and efficiently mill through your entire library, using a combination of cards like Balustrade Spy and Undercity Informer, and then either win the game with Thassa's Oracle or Dread Return a powerful creature from the graveyard. The deck relies heavily on fast mana accelerators like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide to help cast spells quickly, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy with cards like Cabal Therapy and Thoughtseize. The goal is to assemble your combo pieces and win the game before your opponent has a chance to respond.

The deck focuses on utilizing Eldrazi creatures with powerful abilities and cost-reducing lands to create a fast and aggressive playstyle. By utilizing cards like Eldrazi Temple and Eye of Ugin, the deck can quickly ramp into powerful threats like Reality Smasher and Thought-Knot Seer. Chalice of the Void and Wasteland help disrupt opponents' game plans, while cards like Kozilek's Command provide additional removal and utility. With a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells, this deck aims to overwhelm opponents quickly and efficiently in order to secure a victory.

The main concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to quickly ramp up and play powerful Eldrazi creatures, disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Wasteland. By utilizing Eldrazi Temple and Eye of Ugin to reduce costs and search for key creatures, the deck aims to apply consistent pressure and close out the game quickly with aggressive attackers like Reality Smasher and Glaring Fleshraker. The deck also includes some larger Eldrazi threats like It That Heralds the End for late-game dominance.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and artifacts synergies to generate mana quickly and efficiently, while also manipulating the top of the library with cards like Mystic Forge and Manifold Key. The goal is to assemble the combo pieces and disrupt opponent's game plan using cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Glaring Fleshraker. The deck aims to win by assembling a powerful board state with artifacts like Urza's Tower and The One Ring, to overwhelm the opponent and control the game's pace.
